Summary: EP-1 — Offline Routing Engine Foundation
Description:
GOAL: Driver can navigate a pre-downloaded route end-to-end with zero connectivity with truck restrictions enforced.

PRD COVERAGE: FR-3, FR-4

SCOPE IN: OSM bundle format and packaging pipeline. Backend route export API. FleetMate truck restriction overlay (height, weight, tonnage bans). Local routing engine in driver app: turn-by-turn voice and visual, speed limits, ETA recalculation, deviation re-routing within bundle. CEE coverage: Poland, Germany, Czech Republic minimum. CFR validation on device pool.

SCOPE OUT: Download trigger and UI (EP-2). Connectivity indicators (EP-3). HERE SDK (Phase 2). Hazmat routing accuracy parity. Corridor or region-level bundles.

DEPENDENCIES: MOBILE — engine integration. BACKEND — route export API and truck restriction overlay. PLATFORM — local storage.

SUCCESS METRIC: Offline Session Success Rate >=90% of offline-initiated sessions complete (measured in EP-5 pilot).

RISKS: Multi-country bundle may exceed +150MB — validate Sprint 1. Sub-2GB RAM CFR degradation — mandatory post-launch monitoring segment.
Labels: initiative_offline-navigation-mode source_cursor ep_EP-1 fr_FR-3 fr_FR-4 prio_must

Summary: EP-2 — Offline Route Download Pipeline
Description:
GOAL: Route bundles are downloaded automatically on dispatcher assignment and invalidated on cancellation — no driver action required.

PRD COVERAGE: FR-1, FR-2, FR-6

SCOPE IN: Dispatcher-to-device push trigger API. Background download manager (non-blocking, survives app backgrounding). Download progress indicator on route card. Offline-ready confirmation before departure. Download failure error state with retry. Cache invalidation on route cancellation or reassignment. Manual clear offline data in settings. Automated bundle deletion 24h after route completion.

SCOPE OUT: Navigation engine (EP-1). Navigation screen connectivity indicator (EP-3). Corridor/region-level downloads (Phase 2).

DEPENDENCIES: MOBILE — download manager, route card UI. BACKEND — push trigger API (new), bundle generation, cache invalidation. PLATFORM — storage management.

SUCCESS METRIC: Bundle download completion rate >=85% of auto-triggered attempts within 3 minutes.

RISKS: Push trigger is new Backend capability — confirm per-device targeting at story breakdown. Rural 3G download time may exceed 90s target.
Labels: initiative_offline-navigation-mode source_cursor ep_EP-2 fr_FR-1 fr_FR-2 fr_FR-6 prio_must

Summary: EP-3 — Driver Offline UX & Status Layer
Description:
GOAL: Driver always knows their offline mode state through a clear non-intrusive indicator — no ambiguity about route availability.

PRD COVERAGE: FR-5

SCOPE IN: Three-state connectivity status indicator (Online / Offline+Downloaded / Offline+NoRoute). Offline-ready route card badge. Non-blocking mid-trip route invalidation notification. Sub-2GB RAM performance warning on first offline session. Large-font high-contrast design — no driver interaction required.

SCOPE OUT: Download progress indicator (EP-2). Dispatcher-side offline indicators (Phase 2). Navigation UI changes (EP-1).

DEPENDENCIES: DESIGN — indicator design and route card states. MOBILE — implementation. EP-1 required for real connectivity states.

SUCCESS METRIC: 0 pilot drivers report confusion about offline status in pilot debrief.

RISKS: Indicator must not overlap safety-critical navigation elements — mandatory design review before implementation.

Summary: EP-4 — Offline Observability & Instrumentation
Description:
GOAL: Offline Session Success Rate is measured and visible from day one of the pilot.

PRD COVERAGE: FR-8

SCOPE IN: Offline session event schema (started/completed/aborted). Event emission from driver app. Backend event ingestion. Internal dashboard: Offline Session Success Rate overall and segmented by fleet, driver, device RAM tier. Per-participant pilot monitoring view. Dashboard live within 2 weeks of pilot start.

SCOPE OUT: Fleet owner-facing analytics (Phase 2). Battery consumption dashboard.

DEPENDENCIES: DATA — pipeline and dashboard. MOBILE — event emission. BACKEND — event ingestion API.

SUCCESS METRIC: Dashboard live and validated by test session replay before pilot day one.

RISKS: System abort vs voluntary exit must be agreed before implementation — incorrect schema makes all KPI data unreliable.

Summary: EP-5 — Pilot Enablement & GA Readiness
Description:
GOAL: German fleet pilot runs on 10 drivers, written renewal sign-off obtained, and offline navigation reaches GA for all Growth-tier company driver accounts.

PRD COVERAGE: FR-7

SCOPE IN: Fleet-level feature flag for pilot cohort (CS-operable without deployment). Growth-tier eligibility gate (company drivers only, Growth/Scale tier only). Pilot cohort management. Post-pilot sign-off workflow. GA release configuration (server-side, no new app binary required).

SCOPE OUT: User-facing feature toggle. A/B testing infrastructure. Starter-tier offline access. Owner-operator access in Phase 1.

DEPENDENCIES: BACKEND — feature flag, eligibility gate. MOBILE — eligibility gate enforcement. DATA — per-participant monitoring (EP-4). CUSTOMER SUCCESS — pilot coordination and sign-off. German fleet account team — external. EP-1 through EP-4 must all be complete.

SUCCESS METRIC: >=8 of 10 pilot drivers complete >=1 offline session. Written renewal sign-off obtained.

RISKS: German fleet pilot delay is external dependency — CS must secure written schedule 3+ weeks before pilot start. CS owner currently unassigned.

Summary: US-EP1-01 — Offline route bundle generation API
Description:
USER STORY: As the FleetMate system, I need to generate a downloadable offline route bundle for any assigned route so that the driver app can navigate without network connectivity.

SCOPE: Bundle includes route geometry, turn-by-turn instructions, speed limits, truck restriction overlay (height, weight, tonnage bans). No hazmat data in Phase 1. Coverage: Poland, Germany, Czech Republic minimum. Versioned format must be agreed with Mobile before US-EP1-02 and US-EP2-01 begin.

PRD: FR-3, FR-4

DEPENDENCIES: None — defines bundle format contract for all other EP-1 and EP-2 stories. Must be complete before US-EP1-02 and US-EP2-01 begin.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given a route is assigned: When API called with route ID, Then bundle produced containing route geometry, instructions, speed limits, and truck restriction overlay.
- Given a 500km CEE route: When bundle size measured, Then total does not exceed +150MB from baseline (target <=+118MB).
- Given bundle generated: When format version checked, Then it conforms to agreed Mobile/Backend bundle format spec published at Sprint 1.
- Given restriction data unavailable for a segment: When bundle generated, Then missing segment defaults to standard routing and is logged without blocking generation.

OPEN QUESTION: Pre-generated and cached vs on-demand generation? Confirm Backend architecture before story is pointed.
Labels: initiative_offline-navigation-mode source_cursor ep_EP-1 fr_FR-3 fr_FR-4 prio_must

Summary: US-EP1-02 — Offline turn-by-turn navigation
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want to navigate my assigned route with turn-by-turn instructions even when my phone has no mobile signal, so that I can complete my trip on time without relying on connectivity in rural or industrial areas.

SCOPE: Full offline navigation session: turn-by-turn voice and visual, speed limit display, ETA recalculation, deviation re-routing within bundle. Zero network requests from session start to destination. Does not cover download trigger (EP-2) or status indicator (EP-3). Research: drivers check app reliability before long-haul trips — one failure destroys trust.

PRD: FR-3

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP1-01 (bundle format must be defined). PLATFORM for local storage read access.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver has offline-ready route: When navigation starts with zero connectivity, Then turn-by-turn voice and visual instructions provided without any network call.
- Given driver deviates within bundle corridor: When deviation detected, Then app re-routes within 5 seconds using local data.
- Given driver navigating offline: When speed limit present in bundle, Then displayed correctly on navigation screen.
- Given driver navigating offline: When 10 minutes from destination, Then ETA recalculated locally and shown without a network call.
- Given full 500km test route: When session completes, Then CFR on test device (>=2GB RAM) not degraded vs pre-EP-1 baseline.

Summary: US-EP1-03 — Offline truck restriction enforcement
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want my route to respect truck restrictions offline so that I do not accidentally take a restricted road when I have no signal.

SCOPE: Enforces standard height, weight, and tonnage ban restrictions from FleetMate truck restriction overlay. Hazmat routing accuracy is an accepted Phase 1 gap — not in scope. Offline restrictions must be consistent with online routing for the same vehicle profile.

PRD: FR-4

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP1-01 (truck restriction overlay in bundle). US-EP1-02 (engine integrated).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given route includes height or weight restricted segments: When driver navigates offline, Then engine routes around restriction consistent with online behaviour for same vehicle profile.
- Given road with tonnage ban in corridor: When driver navigates offline through that corridor, Then tonnage-banned road excluded and alternative used if available in bundle.
- Given vehicle height exceeds a restriction: When offline bundle navigated, Then restricted road avoided and compliant alternative routed.

OPEN QUESTION: Should a visible disclaimer be shown at offline session start informing driver that hazmat restrictions are not enforced in Phase 1? Requires Legal and CS input before pilot launch.

Summary: US-EP2-01 — Auto-triggered download on route assignment
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want my route to be downloaded automatically when the dispatcher assigns it to me so that I do not have to remember to download it before I lose signal.

SCOPE: Primary activation model. Dispatcher assigns route in web interface, push signal sent to driver device, bundle download begins automatically in background. No driver action required. Research context: prototype test showed 2/5 drivers completed manual download vs 5/5 for auto-trigger.

PRD: FR-1

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP1-01 (bundle must exist). Backend dispatcher-to-device push trigger API (new). Route assignment event as trigger source.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given dispatcher assigns route: When assignment confirmed, Then push trigger sent to driver device and background download begins within 30 seconds without driver action.
- Given driver device is offline at time of assignment: When device reconnects, Then download begins within 60 seconds of reconnection.
- Given driver already has cached bundle for route: When new bundle triggered by route update, Then new bundle replaces old one.

OPEN QUESTION: Does existing push infrastructure support per-device driver targeting? Must be confirmed at Sprint 1 story breakdown — this is a hard blocking dependency.

Summary: US-EP2-02 — Background download with progress indicator
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want to see that my route is downloading in the background without interrupting what I am doing in the app so that I know offline mode is being prepared without effort.

SCOPE: Download runs in background and survives app backgrounding (e.g. incoming call). Non-intrusive progress indicator on route card. Must not interrupt navigation, messaging, or other app functions. Design required.

PRD: FR-2

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP2-01 (download must be triggered). PLATFORM (local storage write).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given download triggered: When driver opens route card, Then progress indicator visible showing download is in progress.
- Given download in progress and driver backgrounds app: When driver returns to FleetMate, Then download continued in background and progress indicator reflects current state.
- Given download in progress and driver starts navigating another route: When navigation active, Then background download continues without interruption.
- Given download completes while route card visible: When download finishes, Then progress indicator transitions to Offline Ready state without driver action.

DESIGN: Component update to route card. Must be legible in direct sunlight. Must not overlap navigation CTA buttons. No full-screen loaders or modals. Figma: TBD.

Summary: US-EP2-03 — Offline Ready confirmation on route card
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want to see a clear confirmation that my route is ready for offline navigation before I start driving so that I can depart with confidence even if I expect to lose signal.

SCOPE: Persistent Offline Ready badge on route card confirming bundle is fully downloaded and valid. Final confirmed state — distinct from download progress indicator (US-EP2-02). Must be visible before driver taps Start navigation.

PRD: FR-2

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP2-02 (download must complete before this state appears).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given bundle fully downloaded: When driver views route card, Then Offline Ready badge persistently visible without requiring interaction.
- Given route shown as Offline Ready: When driver starts navigation with no connectivity, Then offline navigation begins successfully using downloaded bundle.
- Given cached bundle becomes stale (route updated by dispatcher): When new bundle not yet complete, Then Offline Ready badge removed and replaced with progress state until new bundle is complete.

DESIGN: Component update to route card. Must be distinguishable from progress state. High contrast. No tap required to display. Figma: TBD.

Summary: US-EP2-04 — Download failure state and retry
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want to be clearly informed if my offline route download failed so that I can take action before I lose signal rather than discovering the problem mid-trip.

SCOPE: Route card shows clear error state with retry option on download failure. Error message must be actionable in plain language. Must not block Start navigation — online navigation always available.

PRD: FR-2

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP2-01, US-EP2-02.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given download fails due to network error: When download fails, Then route card shows failure state with one-tap retry option within 5 seconds.
- Given download fails due to insufficient storage: When driver sees error, Then message includes plain-language guidance to free up storage.
- Given driver taps Retry with connectivity: When retry initiated, Then download restarts and progress indicator shown.
- Given download failed: When driver taps Start navigation, Then online navigation starts normally — failure does not block the trip.

DESIGN: Component update to route card. Plain language error messages. Large tap target for retry — accessible to gloved hands. No technical error codes visible to drivers. Figma: TBD.

Summary: US-EP2-05 — Cache invalidation on route cancellation or reassignment
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want stale offline route data to be automatically removed from my device when my route changes so that I never accidentally navigate using an outdated bundle.

SCOPE: Push invalidation signal on route cancellation/reassignment removes stale bundle from device within 60 seconds. If driver actively navigating cancelled route offline, notification queued for next safe moment. Automated cleanup 24h after route completion.

PRD: FR-6

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP2-01 (download pipeline). Backend cache invalidation push signal. PLATFORM storage management.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver has offline bundle for a route: When dispatcher cancels route, Then cache invalidation signal pushed and bundle removed from local storage within 60 seconds.
- Given driver has offline bundle: When dispatcher reassigns route to different driver, Then original driver bundle invalidated and removed; new driver begins fresh download.
- Given driver has completed route: When 24 hours have passed since completion, Then offline bundle automatically deleted.
- Given driver actively navigating offline: When dispatcher cancels route mid-trip, Then notification queued and displayed at next moment driver is not in active manoeuvre.

Summary: US-EP2-06 — Manual offline storage management
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want to be able to manually clear my offline route data from app settings so that I can free up device storage when I need to.

SCOPE: Clear offline data option in driver app settings removes all cached route bundles. Does not affect active trip navigation. Secondary fallback — auto-invalidation (US-EP2-05) is primary mechanism.

PRD: FR-2

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP2-01, US-EP2-05.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver has offline bundles downloaded: When they open app settings and tap Offline data, Then total offline storage used (in MB) and Clear offline data option are visible.
- Given driver taps Clear offline data and confirms: When deletion confirmed, Then all cached bundles removed and storage usage returns to zero.
- Given driver clears offline data: When previously downloaded route viewed, Then Offline Ready badge removed and route shows no offline status.

DESIGN: Existing screen update (app settings — add Offline data section). Show storage used before deletion. Confirmation step before deleting. Must not delete data for routes in active navigation. Figma: TBD.

Summary: US-EP3-01 — Three-state connectivity status indicator
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver, I want to see a clear always-visible indicator of my connectivity and offline route status during navigation so that I always know whether my offline route is protecting me.

SCOPE: Three states: (1) Online — visually subdued. (2) Offline with route downloaded — positive reassuring confirmation. (3) Offline with no downloaded route — warning state. Persistent during navigation, non-interactive, legible in direct sunlight. Research: drivers operate under cognitive load and cannot look away from the road — state must communicate in under 1 second.

PRD: FR-5

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP1-02 (real connectivity states from engine). EP-3 design must be complete before implementation.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver navigating with live connection: When navigation active, Then indicator in state 1 (online) — visually subdued.
- Given driver navigating offline with bundle active: When navigation active, Then indicator in state 2 (offline + downloaded) — clearly visible and reassuring.
- Given driver loses connectivity without downloaded bundle: When offline, Then indicator transitions to state 3 (offline no route) — warning state distinguishable from state 2.
- Given device transitions from offline to online mid-trip: When connectivity restored, Then indicator returns to state 1 within 10 seconds.
- Given any state active: When viewed in simulated direct sunlight, Then passes WCAG AA contrast ratio.

DESIGN: New component (persistent navigation status indicator). Must not overlap turn arrows, road names, or speed limit display. Minimum font and icon size per driver app conventions. WCAG AA contrast required. No driver interaction at any time. Figma: TBD.

Summary: US-EP3-02 — Sub-2GB RAM device performance warning
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver on a low-spec Android device, I want to be warned if my device may experience degraded offline navigation performance so that I can make an informed decision before relying on offline mode for a critical trip.

SCOPE: One-time warning on first offline navigation attempt on devices with <2GB RAM. Must not block access to offline navigation. Research: many CEE drivers use budget Android devices with <2GB RAM.

PRD: FR-5

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP1-02 (offline navigation session must exist). Device RAM detection logic.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver device has <2GB RAM: When driver starts first offline session, Then one-time performance warning displayed before navigation starts.
- Given driver dismisses warning: When they start subsequent offline sessions, Then warning not shown again on that device.
- Given driver dismisses warning and proceeds: When offline navigation starts, Then navigation starts normally — warning does not block or delay session.
- Given driver device has >=2GB RAM: When they start offline session, Then no performance warning displayed.

DESIGN: Component update (one-time warning modal or banner before first offline session on qualifying devices). Auto-dismiss after first occurrence. Plain language — no technical terms like RAM. Must not block Start navigation button. Figma: TBD.

Summary: US-EP3-03 — Mid-trip route invalidation notification
Description:
USER STORY: As a company driver navigating offline, I want to be notified if my route has been cancelled or changed while I am driving so that I can contact my dispatcher at the next safe opportunity.

SCOPE: Non-blocking in-app notification when dispatcher cancels or reassigns route while driver is navigating offline. Does not interrupt active manoeuvre. Does not stop navigation. Push signal comes from US-EP2-05 — this story covers in-app presentation logic and UI only.

PRD: FR-5, FR-6

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP2-05 (cache invalidation push signal). US-EP3-01 (indicator state may update).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver navigating offline and dispatcher cancels route: When driver is not within 200m of a turn instruction, Then non-blocking banner notification appears within 60 seconds of cancellation signal reaching device.
- Given notification appears: When 8 seconds pass without driver interaction, Then notification auto-dismisses.
- Given driver within 200m of a turn: When route invalidation signal arrives, Then notification held and displayed after manoeuvre is complete.
- Given driver taps notification: When tapped, Then taken to route card — navigation does not stop.

DESIGN: Component update (in-navigation non-blocking notification banner). Must not overlap turn arrow, road name, or speed limit display. No modal. Auto-dismisses after 8-10 seconds. Large legible text for brief glance. Must not appear during active manoeuvre (within 200m of turn). Figma: TBD.
Labels: initiative_offline-navigation-mode source_cursor ep_EP-3 fr_FR-5 fr_FR-6 prio_must

Summary: US-EP4-01 — Offline session event emission
Description:
USER STORY: As a product team, I need the driver app to emit structured events at each offline navigation session state transition so that Offline Session Success Rate can be accurately calculated.

SCOPE: Three events required: offline_session_started (navigation begins with zero connectivity), offline_session_completed (driver reaches destination), offline_session_aborted (system-forced exit before destination — NOT voluntary driver exit). Each event includes: driver ID, fleet ID, device model, RAM tier (>=2GB vs <2GB), route ID, timestamp, session duration.

PRD: FR-8

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP1-02 (offline navigation session must exist). Event schema agreement with Data (INT-02 must be complete before implementation begins).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver starts navigation with zero connectivity: When session begins, Then offline_session_started event emitted within 5 seconds containing driver ID, fleet ID, device model, RAM tier, route ID, timestamp.
- Given driver reaches destination: When destination confirmed, Then offline_session_completed event emitted with session ID and duration.
- Given session ends due to system failure: When session ends abnormally, Then offline_session_aborted event emitted with session ID, abort reason code, and duration at time of abort.
- Given driver voluntarily exits offline navigation: When driver taps exit, Then NO offline_session_aborted event is emitted.

OPEN QUESTION: Is voluntary exit reliably distinguishable from system abort in existing session lifecycle? If not, Mobile must add explicit abort_type field. Must be resolved before implementation — incorrect schema makes all Phase 1 KPI data unreliable.

Summary: US-EP4-02 — Offline Session Success Rate dashboard
Description:
USER STORY: As a product manager, I need an internal dashboard showing Offline Session Success Rate segmented by fleet, driver, and device so that I can monitor Phase 1 performance and validate the 90% target hypothesis.

SCOPE: Dashboard shows Offline Session Success Rate (% = completed / (completed + aborted)), session volume (started/completed/aborted), segmented views by fleet/driver/device RAM tier. Daily refresh acceptable. Internal product and CS team access. Must be live before German fleet pilot starts.

PRD: FR-8

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP4-01 (events must be emitted). Backend event ingestion API.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given offline session events being emitted: When Data pipeline ingests events, Then dashboard shows Offline Session Success Rate updated within 24 hours.
- Given dashboard open: When sessions segmented by fleet, Then each fleet individual success rate visible with session volume.
- Given dashboard open: When sessions segmented by device RAM tier, Then >=2GB and <2GB segments shown separately.
- Given controlled offline test session run successfully: When dashboard checked following day, Then test session appears as completed and counted in success rate.

Summary: US-EP4-03 — Per-participant pilot monitoring view
Description:
USER STORY: As a Customer Success manager, I need a per-driver view of offline session outcomes during the German fleet pilot so that I can monitor whether the pilot meets renewal acceptance criteria and identify drivers having issues.

SCOPE: Per-participant view showing each pilot driver name/ID, offline sessions started/completed/aborted, individual Offline Session Success Rate. CS-accessible. Daily refresh acceptable. Must be live before pilot day one.

PRD: FR-8, FR-7

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP4-02 (dashboard must exist). EP-5 pilot cohort must be configured so pilot driver IDs are known.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given German fleet pilot cohort configured: When CS monitor opens per-participant view, Then each of 10 pilot drivers listed with offline session count (started/completed/aborted) and individual success rate.
- Given pilot driver completes offline session: When dashboard checked following day, Then session appears under that driver row.
- Given pilot driver has zero sessions in pilot window: When CS monitor views dashboard, Then driver appears with zero count and is not omitted from the view.
Labels: initiative_offline-navigation-mode source_cursor ep_EP-4 fr_FR-8 fr_FR-7 prio_must

Summary: US-EP5-01 — Fleet-level feature flag for pilot cohort
Description:
USER STORY: As a Customer Success manager, I need to enable offline navigation for specific driver accounts within a single fleet without affecting any other users so that I can run the German fleet controlled pilot.

SCOPE: Fleet-level or driver-level feature flag enabling offline nav for defined driver accounts. CS-operable without engineering deployment. Supports adding/removing drivers without new app release. Independent of Growth-tier eligibility gate (US-EP5-02).

PRD: FR-7

DEPENDENCIES: EP-1 through EP-4 complete before pilot can begin. Feature flag infrastructure must support driver-level granularity (open question).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given CS adds driver to pilot cohort: When driver opens app, Then offline navigation features active for that driver.
- Given driver is in pilot cohort and dispatcher assigns route: When route assigned, Then auto-triggered download initiates for that driver.
- Given driver removed from pilot cohort: When they open app, Then offline navigation features no longer visible or active.
- Given 10 pilot drivers in cohort: When any user outside cohort opens app, Then they see no offline navigation features.

OPEN QUESTION: Does existing feature flag infrastructure support driver-level granularity? If only fleet-level supported, confirm with German fleet account team whether enabling offline for all their drivers during pilot is acceptable.

Summary: US-EP5-02 — Growth-tier eligibility gate enforcement
Description:
USER STORY: As a product manager, I need offline navigation to be accessible only to Growth-tier fleet accounts with employed company drivers so that the feature is correctly positioned as a Growth-tier differentiator.

SCOPE: Eligibility conditions: (1) driver linked fleet is Growth or Scale tier. (2) driver account type is company driver (employed) — not owner-operator. Gate enforced server-side (API rejects ineligible bundle requests) and client-side (UI elements not rendered for ineligible users). Starter-tier and owner-operator accounts see no offline features.

PRD: FR-7

DEPENDENCIES: Account tier and driver type available in driver app auth context. US-EP5-01 pilot flag operates independently of this gate.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given driver linked to Growth-tier fleet with company driver account type: When driver opens app, Then offline navigation features available.
- Given driver linked to Starter-tier fleet: When driver opens app, Then no offline navigation UI visible and no bundle downloads triggered.
- Given driver has owner-operator account type: When driver opens app, Then no offline navigation UI visible regardless of fleet tier.
- Given ineligible driver device attempts bundle download API request: When API receives request, Then request rejected with appropriate error code — no bundle served.
- Given US-EP5-02 deployed to staging: When Starter-tier and owner-operator test accounts tested, Then both confirm zero visibility of offline features (mandatory exit criterion before pilot launch).

Summary: US-EP5-03 — Post-pilot GA release configuration
Description:
USER STORY: As a product manager, I need to release offline navigation to all eligible Growth-tier company driver accounts after the German fleet pilot sign-off so that the commercial obligation is fulfilled and the feature reaches its full audience.

SCOPE: After German fleet pilot meets written acceptance criteria and sign-off obtained, offline navigation released to GA for all Growth-tier fleet accounts with company driver account types. GA release must be server-side configuration — no new app binary required.

PRD: FR-7

DEPENDENCIES: US-EP5-01 (pilot complete and signed off). US-EP5-02 (eligibility gate live). EP-4 dashboard showing pilot session data. Written renewal sign-off from German fleet account team.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given pilot completed minimum 2-week window and written criteria met: When Sergio Barguilla approves GA release, Then server-side configuration updated and offline navigation available to all Growth-tier company driver accounts.
- Given GA configuration live: When Growth-tier company driver opens app, Then offline navigation features available without app update required.
- Given GA configuration live: When Starter-tier driver or owner-operator opens app, Then no offline navigation features visible — eligibility gate remains enforced.
- Given GA configuration deployed: When Offline Session Success Rate dashboard checked 24h post-GA, Then session events from non-pilot Growth-tier drivers appearing confirming successful rollout.

Summary: NFR-01 — CFR monitoring on sub-2GB RAM devices post-launch
Description:
STATEMENT: After Phase 1 GA release, Crash-Free Rate on driver app sessions from devices with <2GB RAM must be monitored separately from overall CFR. If sub-2GB RAM CFR drops below 98.5% in any weekly review, a device-specific mitigation must be investigated and shipped within one sprint.

RELATED TO: EP-1, EP-3, FR-3

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given Phase 1 GA live: When crash monitoring data reviewed weekly, Then <2GB RAM device CFR reported as a separate segment from >=2GB CFR.
- Given sub-2GB RAM CFR drops below 98.5%: When threshold breached, Then engineering lead notified and mitigation scoped for following sprint.

Summary: NFR-02 — Offline bundle download performance monitoring
Description:
STATEMENT: Offline route bundle download time must be measured and reported in the first 4 weeks post-GA. If p75 download time for a 500km CEE route exceeds 3 minutes on a 4G connection, a compression or progressive download investigation must be initiated.

RELATED TO: EP-2, FR-2

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given GA live and auto-triggered downloads occurring: When download performance data reviewed after 4 weeks, Then p50 and p75 download completion times available segmented by connection type (4G, 3G, Wi-Fi).
- Given p75 download time exceeds 3 minutes for representative route: When threshold breached, Then Mobile and Backend leads initiate bundle compression investigation in following sprint.

Summary: INT-01 — Offline bundle format contract (Mobile and Backend)
Description:
STATEMENT: Before US-EP1-02 and US-EP2-01 can be built in parallel, Mobile and Backend must agree and publish a formal offline bundle format contract covering structure, versioning, field definitions, and validation rules. Must be produced at end of Sprint 1.

DEPENDS ON: US-EP1-01 (Backend defines format). Mobile engineering lead review and sign-off required.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given Sprint 1 complete: When bundle format spec reviewed by both Mobile and Backend leads, Then versioned spec document published to initiative folder and both leads have confirmed it.
- Given spec published: When US-EP1-02 and US-EP2-01 begin, Then both stories reference the agreed spec — no independent assumptions about bundle structure.

Summary: INT-02 — Offline session event schema agreement (Mobile and Data)
Description:
STATEMENT: Before US-EP4-01 can be implemented, Mobile and Data must agree on the exact event schema for offline_session_started, offline_session_completed, and offline_session_aborted — including the definition of system abort vs voluntary exit. Agreement must be documented before any instrumentation sprint begins.

DEPENDS ON: Unblocks US-EP4-01 and US-EP4-02.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Given Sprint 1 complete: When Mobile and Data have reviewed session lifecycle for abort vs voluntary exit differentiation, Then formal event schema document published with field definitions, enum values for abort type, and agreed session lifecycle mapping.
- Given schema published: When US-EP4-01 implemented, Then all three events conform exactly to the agreed schema — no deviations.
